<insights>

- High cohesion (0.86) — files are tightly interconnected
- Guard commands/policy already have unit and integration coverage—extend those tests before shipping new guardrails.
- Share paths/contracts with the core Hench zone deliberately since they are mutually dependent.
- Contracts/policy modules form the safety net for Hench, so evolve validation logic here rather than duplicating checks downstream.
- Bidirectional imports with the main Hench zone mean any guard change can ripple; document required invariants in `contracts.ts` to prevent cycles.
